name: Neynar API Vocabulary
description: Vocabulary terms extracted from Neynar Farcaster API and Hub API OpenAPI specifications

terms.description: Operations related to user
Operations related to signer
Operations related to cast
Operations related to feed

Operations related to mini app host notifications

Legacy event type corresponding to the requested event type:
- frame_added: User adds a mini app to their account
- frame_removed: User removes a mini app from their account
- notifications_enabled: User enables notifications for a mini app
- notifications_disabled: User disables notifications for a mini app
Details for notification setup, only present when event is notifications_enabled
List of domains for which notifications are enabled for this user
The unique identifier of a farcaster user or app (unsigned integer)

Number of storage units to buy.
A storage unit lets you store 5000 casts, 2500 reactions and 2500 links.

Positions within the text (inclusive start, exclusive end) where each mention occurs.
Each index within this list corresponds to the same-numbered index in the mentioned_channels list.

Positions within the text (inclusive start, exclusive end) where each mention occurs.
Each index within this list corresponds to the same-numbered index in the mentioned_profiles list.
note: This is recursive. It contains the direct replies to the cast and their direct replies up to n reply_depth.
This has been deprecated and will always be an empty array. The property will be removed in the future
This has been deprecated and will always be an empty array. The property will be removed in the future
Indicates if the viewer liked the cast.
Indicates if the viewer recasted the cast.
Positions within the text (inclusive start, exclusive end) where each mention occurs.
Channel's external link.
Number of followers the channel has.
Use `lead` instead.
Indicates if the user is following the channel.

Factory name
- wow -> [wow.xyz](https://wow.xyz)
- clanker -> [clanker.world](https://www.clanker.world)
Description of the token
Discord server link

Media file associated with the token. 
Supported formats are image/jpeg, image/gif and image/png
Indicates if the token is NSFW (Not Safe For Work).
Telegram link
Twitter profile link
Website link related to the token
Network/Chain name
Ethereum address of the one who is creating the token
Symbol/Ticker for the token
The base URL of the app on which the action is being performed.
Index of the button
Used specifically for the tx action type to post a successful transaction hash
Target of the button
Title of the button
Launch URL of the mini app
Version of the mini app, 'next' for v2, 'vNext' for v1
The number of decimals the token uses
The logo URL of the token
The total supply of the token
The radius in meters for the location search. Any location within this radius will be returned.
Domain name
High-level error message if processing failed
Home URL metadata if available
Last update timestamp
Validation errors from processing, if any
Whether the domain was found in the table before processing
Whether the domain was refreshed/updated during this request
The nonce value to sign the message
The body text of the notification.
When the campaign reached a terminal state (completed/failed/canceled). Null while still queued or running.
When the campaign was enqueued for async delivery.
Detailed breakdown of errors encountered during notification delivery.
An array of Farcaster FIDs of intended recipient applications.
The total number of notification tokens for intended recipients.
The number of notifications successfully sent.
A record mapping app FIDs (as strings) to the number of successful sends for that app.
The total number of times notifications from this campaign have been opened.
A record mapping app FIDs (as strings) to the number of opens for that app.
The number of unique recipients who opened a notification from this campaign.
A record mapping app FIDs (as strings) to the number of unique opens for that app.
The name of the author/owner of the resource.
A URL for the author/owner of the resource.
The suggested cache lifetime for this resource, in seconds. Consumers may choose to use this value or not.
The name of the resource provider.
The url of the resource provider.
The height of the optional thumbnail. If this parameter is present, thumbnail_url and thumbnail_width must also be present.

A URL to a thumbnail image representing the resource. The thumbnail must respect any maxwidth and maxheight parameters. If this parameter is present, thumbnail_width and thumbnail_height must also be present.
The width of the optional thumbnail. If this parameter is present, thumbnail_url and thumbnail_height must also be present.
The unique identifier for the notification campaign.
unix timestamp in seconds that controls how long the signed key request is valid for. (24 hours from now is recommended)

Url to redirect to after the signer is approved.
**Note** : This should only be used when requesting a signer from a native mobile application.
Accept or reject the invite
Filters to apply to the target_fids set. All filters are additive, so only users matching all filters will be notified.
The number of notifications that failed to deliver.
The number of notifications not attempted (e.g., disabled tokens, invalid tokens).
List of FIDs that failed due to retryable errors (rate_limited, failed, http_error). Can be used to retry sending notifications to these users.
The number of notifications successfully delivered.

Neynar will sponsor the signer if set to true.
**Note: ** If sponsor.fid and sponsor.signature are provided along with sponsored_by_neynar set to true,
the sponsor.fid and sponsor.signature will be ignored.
Neynar will sponsor the signer on behalf of the user. The developer will get charged in credits.
Domain of the mini app
JFS-signed message containing the event payload. The message must be properly signed and contain valid event information.  Can be provided as a single string or json object.
Custom button color for action button
Custom text color for action button
Optional list of FIDs that are allowed to use this transaction mini app
List of items included in the transaction
Amount to send (must be greater than 0)
Token contract address for the payment (e.g. 0x833589fcd6edb6e08f4c7c32d4f71b54bda02913 is USDC on Base)
Reason for failure (if status is failed)
Contract address of the fungible token to send. If not provided, the default is the native token of the network.
Hash of the transaction that approved the transfer. This is only present if the fungible token is not native token of the network.
Gas used for the transaction.
Up to five recent Farcaster users who posted about the topic
A URL-friendly unique identifier for the topic
Requires pro subscription.
Must be one of the verified addresses.
Must be one of the verified addresses.
The number of users the user is following.
Score that represents the probability that the account is not spam.
Indicates if the viewer is blocked by the user.
Indicates if the viewer is blocking the user.
Indicates if the viewer is followed by the user.
ISO timestamp when the verification was created
Filter for casts that contain embedded casts authored by these FIDs
Filter for casts that quote/embed these specific cast hashes

Exclude casts that matches these authors.
**Note:**
This is applied as an AND operation against rest of the filters.
Rest of the filters are bundled as an OR operation.

Base64-encoded pagination token for fetching the next page of results. An empty value indicates there are no more pages to return. Used in conjunction with the pageSize parameter to implement pagination across large result sets.
Identifies which button the user clicked in the frame. Frames can have up to 4 buttons, numbered from 1 to 4.
User-defined timestamp that preserves the original creation time when message.data.timestamp needs to be updated for compaction. Useful for maintaining chronological order in user feeds.
The unique identifier of the cast being reacted to. Must be a valid CastId containing the creator's FID and the cast's hash.
The URL being reacted to. Used when reacting to external content or when the cast ID is not available.
List of subscription tiers for the user. Each entry describes a subscription tier and its expiration.
Base64-encoded signature produced by the blockchain address, proving ownership. For Ethereum, this is an ECDSA signature of a specific message format.
